area: MSC 18C50/18B20 (universal coalgebra, categorical semantics of computation), 68Q85 (bisimulation, behavioral equivalences), 03B45 (modal logic) — coalgebra, coinduction, up-to techniques, coalgebraic modal logic expressivity, behavioral metrics
hole_justification: Searches run: web searches for "Collatz coalgebra coinduction", "Collatz bisimulation 3x+1", "Collatz final coalgebra arXiv", "Jiho Kim 3x+1 coalgebra conjugacy", "mathoverflow Collatz coinduction coalgebra", "coinductive 3n+1/3x+1 divergence invariant certificate", "Collatz delay monad / partiality monad", "non-termination up-to congruence tree automata SAT"; a Semantic Scholar API citation query on Kim arXiv:0809.4187 (returned zero citing papers); pdftotext greps of Lagarias's annotated bibliographies I (1963-1999) and II (2000-2009) and his 2021 overview — zero occurrences of "coalgebra", "coinduc-", "bisimul-" across the entire annotated Collatz literature; a grep of Rutten's 2019 textbook "The Method of Coalgebra" — no Collatz; greps of the Endrullis-Zantema RTA 2015 / Zantema 2014 / Wang 2015 nontermination-by-automata papers — zero Collatz mentions. Findings: exactly ONE genuine Collatz-coalgebra paper exists (Jiho Kim 2008, arXiv:0809.4187, companion paper "Coinductive Properties of Causal Maps" AMAST 2008): it proves the 2-adic Collatz map with parity observation is itself a FINAL bitstream coalgebra and re-derives Lagarias's Q-infinity conjugacy as the unique coalgebra isomorphism — purely structural, no convergence/divergence content, and Kim explicitly writes the approach's "utility ... may be limited in terms of resolving the 3x+1 Problem". No follow-ups, no citations found. Up-to techniques (Pous, Bonchi-Pous) have never been applied to Collatz, nor even to Endrullis-Zantema-style regular non-termination certificates in general. Behavioral/bisimulation metrics have never been applied to Collatz-like maps. Verdict: one isolated structural paper = fringe; the certificate-enlargement and metric sub-questions are virgin.

prior_art.ref: Jiho Kim, 'Coinductive properties of Lipschitz functions on streams', arXiv:0809.4187 (2008)
prior_art.what: THE one prior paper. Thm 4.4: the 2-adic Collatz map T with parity observation h is a final coalgebra in the category of 2-stream coalgebras; the unique isomorphism to the stream coalgebra is exactly Lagarias's 3x+1 conjugacy map Q-infinity; also builds a class of 'woven' Collatz-like maps all yielding final coalgebras. Structural only — no orbit convergence/divergence results; author explicitly disclaims direct applicability to the conjecture.

prior_art.ref: J. Endrullis, H. Zantema, 'Proving non-termination by finite automata', RTA 2015
prior_art.what: The (non-Collatz) ancestor of our regular divergence certificates: nonempty regular language closed under rewriting containing no normal forms proves non-termination; automated by SAT over automata of fixed size. Never applied to Collatz maps; no up-to enhancement ever developed for it.

prior_art.ref: H. Zantema, 'Non-termination using Regular Languages' arXiv:1405.5662 (2014); Wang, 'Proving Looping and Non-Looping Non-Termination by Finite Automata' arXiv:1505.00478 (2015)
prior_art.what: Extensions of the regular-certificate method (string rewriting; looping vs non-looping distinction). Still no Collatz, no up-to.

prior_art.ref: F. Bonchi, D. Pous, 'Checking NFA equivalence with bisimulations up to congruence', POPL 2013
prior_art.what: The up-to-congruence technique and HKC algorithm; exhibits families where the up-to-congruence relation is exponentially smaller than any plain bisimulation. This is the 'other side' machinery — never connected to non-termination certificates or number theory.

prior_art.ref: D. Pous, 'Coinduction All the Way Up', LICS 2016
prior_art.what: The companion: largest compatible function; uniform soundness theorem for up-to techniques on any complete lattice — directly applicable to our divergence functional, unused for it.

prior_art.ref: P. Baldan, F. Bonchi, H. Kerstan, B. König, 'Coalgebraic Behavioral Metrics', LMCS 14(3) 2018, arXiv:1712.07511
prior_art.what: Canonical behavioral pseudometrics via Kantorovich/Wasserstein functor liftings; final coalgebra carries a canonical distance. Never applied to arithmetic dynamics.

prior_art.ref: V. Capretta, 'General Recursion via Coinductive Types', LMCS 2005, arXiv:cs/0505037
prior_art.what: Delay monad: divergence of a partial computation is a coinductive predicate — the mechanization-ready (Lean/Coq) semantics of 'T diverges on n'; Collatz appears in this literature only as a definitional example, never with proofs.

prior_art.ref: H. Geuvers, B. Jacobs, 'Relating Apartness and Bisimulation', LMCS 17(3) 2021, arXiv:2002.02512
prior_art.what: Apartness = inductive complement of bisimilarity with a positive derivation system — the right formal tool for finitely witnessing that candidate coinductive certificates FAIL (our nonexistence sweeps produce exactly such witnesses).

prior_art.ref: Lagarias 1985 ('The 3x+1 problem and its generalizations') and Bernstein-Lagarias 1996 ('The 3x+1 conjugacy map')
prior_art.what: The classical results (T on Z_2 conjugate to 2-shift; explicit conjugacy) that Kim 2008 repackages coalgebraically — the arithmetic content the pure behavioral view forgets.

bridge: Objects: a deterministic arithmetic dynamical system with observation is a coalgebra <o,T>: X -> O x X for the functor F = O x (-). Collatz: X = Z_2 (or binary numerals Sigma*), O = 2, o = parity; the final F-coalgebra is the set of bitstreams 2^omega with <head,tail>, and the unique morphism X -> 2^omega is the parity-vector map — Kim 2008 shows <parity,T> is ITSELF final, with Lagarias's conjugacy Q-infinity as the unique coalgebra isomorphism (2-shift conjugacy = finality). Divergence: the set D of divergent points is the greatest fixpoint nu(b) of the monotone functional b(S) = { x not in Done : T(x) in S } on the powerset lattice; a divergence certificate is a post-fixpoint S with n0 in S, S subseteq b(S) — i.e., a coinductive invariant (a unary bisimulation). OUR regular divergence certificates ARE exactly post-fixpoints of b constrained to regular languages of base-2 numerals (T is computed by an LSB-first letter-to-letter transducer, i.e., a Mealy machine / Kim's 'causal map', so all conditions are regular); the exhaustive <=7-DFA-state result = nonexistence of small PLAIN coinductive invariants in that syntactic class. Up-to: for any closure c below the companion t_b (Pous 2016), a post-fixpoint of b compose c ('invariant up to c': T(L) subseteq c(L)) still witnesses containment in nu(b); Bonchi-Pous instantiate c = congruence closure and get invariants exponentially smaller than plain ones. Correspondence table: NFA states <-> numeral strings; NFA transition relation <-> the transducer computing T; bisimulation R <-> regular invariant L; congruence closure c(R) <-> closure of L under a synchronized rational relation R that commutes with T; HKC's SAT-size win <-> exponentially smaller divergence certificates. Metrics: the Kantorovich lifting of F = 2 x (-) induces the canonical behavioral ultrametric d(x,y) = 2^{-(first step where parity vectors of x,y differ)} on the final coalgebra, whose pullback along Q-infinity is (equivalent to) the 2-adic metric; a 'distance between Collatz-like maps' U,V lives one level up, as Hausdorff distance in the hyperspace of closed subsets of the fixed final coalgebra 2^omega between the images Q_U(N) and Q_V(N) of the integers.
strongest_adjacent_theorems: Kim 2008 (arXiv:0809.4187), Thm 4.4: <parity, T> : Z_2 -> 2 x Z_2 is a terminal object in the category of 2-stream coalgebras; the unique isomorphism to bitstreams is Lagarias's Q-infinity — the coalgebraic form of 'T on Z_2 is conjugate to the 2-shift' (Lagarias 1985).

Pous, LICS 2016 ('Coinduction All the Way Up'): for any monotone b on a complete lattice, the companion t_b is the largest compatible function; every f <= t_b yields a sound up-to technique (post-fixpoints of b∘f are below nu(b)), and f <= t_b can itself be proved coinductively (second-order coinduction). Mechanized in Pous's Coq 'coinduction' library.

Bonchi-Pous, POPL 2013: bisimulation up to congruence is sound and complete for NFA language equivalence (HKC algorithm); explicit families (their Figs. 5-6) where the up-to-congruence relation is exponentially smaller than any plain bisimulation — the precise 'up-to certificates can be exponentially smaller' theorem.

Bonchi-Petrisan-Pous-Rot, CSL-LICS 2014 ('Coinduction Up-To in a Fibrational Setting'): up-to techniques generalized from binary bisimulations to arbitrary coinductive PREDICATES in a fibration (including quantitative/metric predicates) — the exact abstract setting of unary divergence invariants like ours.

Endrullis-Zantema, RTA 2015: if a nonempty regular (tree) language is closed under rewriting and contains no normal forms, the system is non-terminating; automated via SAT over fixed-size automata; proved non-termination of the S-combinator where all previous methods failed.

Schröder, TCS 390 (2008) ('Expressivity of coalgebraic modal logic: the limits and beyond'): a finitary coalgebraic modal logic is expressive (Hennessy-Milner: logical indistinguishability = behavioral equivalence) iff the functor admits a separating set of predicate liftings; explicit functors admitting NO expressive unary modal logic — the template theorem for 'observer class X cannot express behavioral property Y'.

Baldan-Bonchi-Kerstan-König, LMCS 14(3) 2018: Kantorovich and Wasserstein liftings of a functor to pseudometric spaces yield canonical behavioral distances; when a final coalgebra exists it carries a canonical behavioral metric generalizing bisimilarity.

Geuvers-Jacobs, LMCS 17(3) 2021: apartness is the inductive dual of bisimilarity — two states are apart iff not bisimilar — with a positive (finite-derivation) proof system; gives finite witnesses of NON-membership in coinductive predicates.

Capretta, LMCS 2005: the delay monad (final coalgebra of X -> A + X) makes 'diverges' a coinductive predicate definable in type theory — the standard mechanized semantics of non-termination (Lean/Coq/Agda).
the_push: (a) Up-to-enlarged certificates — the sharp push. Define an R-up-to regular divergence certificate: a DFA A with language L and a letter-synchronized rational relation R (finite transducer B) such that: n0 in L; L avoids Done; R preserves not-Done; x R y implies T(x) R T(y) (R commutes with the T-transducer); and T(L) subseteq (R ∪ id)(L). Soundness is a one-page companion/compatibility argument (Pous 2016; fibrational version Bonchi-Petrisan-Pous-Rot 2014 since this is a unary predicate, not a relation). This STRICTLY enlarges the searched class, and by the Bonchi-Pous gap phenomenon a (7-state A, k-state B) up-to certificate can dominate plain certificates of exponentially larger DFA size — so our exhaustive <=7-state theorem does NOT close this class; every condition above is regular, hence the search stays SAT-encodable. Honest orientation of the payoff: for 3n+1 itself a divergence certificate would REFUTE the conjecture, so there we expect (and want) stronger nonexistence theorems — 'divergence, if any, admits no small regular-up-to-R witness', an exponential strengthening per unit of compute. The genuinely new-theorem target is the mirror family: for 5n+1 (orbit of 7, divergence believed but OPEN) or 7n+1 or Conway-style maps, one found certificate = an unconditional new divergence theorem that no existing technique can produce. The same companion machinery also applies to convergence-side certificates (Monks-style sufficient semilinear sets closed under congruence): up-to closures shrink those searches too. (b) Behavioral metrics — mostly a principled NEGATIVE insight plus a clean formalization. The canonical behavioral metric for F = 2 x (-) is the parity-stream ultrametric, and by Kim's finality ALL Collatz-like maps in the woven class are behaviorally isomorphic (each realizes the full final coalgebra): state-level behavioral distance provably cannot see the conjecture. This is the precise coalgebraic explanation of why the Lagarias conjugacy is unhelpful — the conjecture lives in how the arithmetic substructure N (an initial-algebra structure) embeds into the final coalgebra, i.e., in the ALGEBRA-coalgebra interplay, not in behavior alone. The non-degenerate 'distance from Collatz': U maps to Q_U(N) subseteq 2^omega, a point of the hyperspace of closed subsets under Hausdorff metric; the sandwich program becomes: study continuity of U -> Q_U(N) along the Bernstein-Lagarias parametrization (provable) and whether 'Q_U(N) contained in the basin of the trivial cycle' has interior around decidable instances. Kurtz-Simon Pi-0-2-completeness caps ambition: no computable metric criterion can globally carve out provable convergence, so only local/relative statements are realistic.
first_move: Lemma 1 (soundness of up-to-R divergence certificates): let b(S) = { x in Sigma* : x not in Done, T(x) in S } on P(Sigma*); if a synchronized rational relation R preserves not-Done and satisfies x R y => T(x) R T(y), then c_R = closure under (R ∪ id) is b-compatible (c_R ∘ b <= b ∘ c_R), hence below the companion, hence any regular L with n0 in L, L ∩ Done = empty, T(L) subseteq (R ∪ id)(L) proves divergence of n0. Prove it on paper (about a page, following Pous LICS 2016 Sec. 3), then formalize in Lean 4 (port the companion pattern from Pous's Coq coinduction library — the lattice is just P(Sigma*)). Immediately after: extend the existing SAT encoding with a second automaton — variables = DFA A (<=7 states) + letter-to-letter transducer B (<=3 states) for R; constraints (all regular): T(L) subseteq (R∪id)(L), R∘T-transducer commutation, Done-avoidance; run on the 32-core machines FIRST against the 5n+1 map with seed 7 (a found certificate is a new open-problem-solving theorem; nonexistence at small sizes is also reportable), then re-run the 3n+1 sweep to upgrade the <=7-state nonexistence theorem to the up-to class.
payoff_odds: Realistic best case (maybe 5-10%): an up-to-regular divergence certificate for a generalized Collatz map whose divergence is open (5n+1 orbit of 7 is the canonical target) — an unconditional new theorem solving a named open problem, plus the framework paper; qx+1 divergence has resisted everything, and divergent 5n+1 parity vectors may simply not be regular-up-to-anything-small, so treat this as a lottery ticket with a real but small chance. Likely case (60-70%): Lemma 1 proved and Lean-formalized cheaply (the companion theory makes it near-routine), the SAT search runs, no certificate found for 5n+1 at reachable sizes, and the program gains (i) a strictly stronger nonexistence theorem for 3n+1 ('no <=7-state regular divergence certificate even up to any <=3-state synchronized rational bisimulation'), which is exponentially stronger than the current result in plain-certificate terms, (ii) the clean degeneracy theorem for behavioral metrics on the parity coalgebra (explaining precisely why conjugacy-level structure cannot decide the conjecture), and (iii) a hyperspace formalization of the sandwich program with a continuity lemma — a solid CALCO/CONCUR/LICS-adjacent methods paper with real Collatz content, but no direct dent in the conjecture. The metric push alone (<10%) yields a new theorem beyond degeneracy + continuity. Failure mode to watch: the compatibility condition on R may be so restrictive over numeral transducers that the up-to class collapses to the plain class at small sizes — even that is a citable closure theorem, but a deflating one.
